common.empty_logs: (empty — start the service to see logs)

config.title: Settings
config.subtitle: Adjust ports and general preferences. Port changes apply on next service start.
config.firewall_desc_html:
By default Windows blocks incoming connections for unknown executables — the first time you start nginx you usually see the "Allow access?" popup. If you click Cancel or nothing prompts, other devices on your LAN (phone, another PC) won't reach your server.

This button creates permanent inbound rules in Windows Firewall authorizing TCP traffic for the three MadiStack binaries (nginx.exe, mysqld.exe, php-cgi.exe). Everything is done in a single UAC prompt — MadiStack doesn't stay elevated, only the helper runs as admin and exits.

You only need to click "Create / Recreate rules" if:

  • You want to reach your .test sites from another device on the same network (phone for responsive testing, etc.)
  • You set Bind address: 0.0.0.0 and the service doesn't respond externally
  • You reinstalled Windows or cleared the rules manually and want them back

For local-only use (127.0.0.1 / localhost), these rules are unnecessary — loopback traffic doesn't pass through the firewall.

mariadb_root_password.label: Password
mariadb_root_password.hint: Use 'root' as the user when connecting from PHP, the CLI or external tools.
mariadb_root_password.not_initialized: MariaDB hasn't been initialized yet. Start the service at least once to generate the password.

sites.title: Sites
sites.subtitle_html: Every subfolder of www/ becomes a site served at <name>.test. Enabling edits the Windows hosts file (asks for UAC) and reloads nginx.
sites.empty_html: No folder in www/ yet. Create one like www/mysite/ with an index.php and refresh this page.
sites.https_toggle_title: Issues cert via mkcert on first use (single UAC prompt).

updates.title: Updates
updates.subtitle: Installed versions vs. latest published by upstream.
updates.unknown_version_tooltip: Binary present in bin/, but MadiStack didn't record the version. Reinstall to sync.
updates.sync_tooltip: Downloads the current version and registers it in MadiStack to enable auto-update.

geral.title: General
geral.subtitle: Service status and controls.
geral.pma_open_tooltip: Open http://localhost:{port}/phpmyadmin/ in the default browser
geral.pma_needs_nginx: Start Nginx first — phpMyAdmin is served by it
geral.pma_mariadb_hint: phpMyAdmin will open, but login only works with MariaDB running
geral.pma_password_warning: ⚠ Initial MariaDB password — change it on your first phpMyAdmin login
geral.onboarding_body_html: Click Download all to fetch nginx, PHP, MariaDB and phpMyAdmin from official sources (first run takes ~2 min). Then, on each row, Start brings the service up. Put your sites in subfolders of www/ and the Sites tab turns each one into <name>.test.

service.open_config_tooltip: Opens {path} in the default Windows editor
service.open_log_tooltip: Opens {path} in the default Windows editor

about.title: About
about.description: is a portable web stack for Windows. It downloads, configures and keeps Nginx, MariaDB, PHP and phpMyAdmin up-to-date from official sources, wrapped in a native GUI built with Rust + Tauri. Every subfolder of www/ becomes a site served at <name>.test, with local HTTPS via mkcert and integrated Windows Firewall management. Everything runs from the executable's folder — no registry install, no resident services, works from a USB drive.
about.author: Author
about.made_in: Made with ❤ in Araçatuba, São Paulo, Brazil 🇧🇷

tour.step1_body: This button fetches nginx, PHP, MariaDB and phpMyAdmin from official sources. First run takes ~2 min.
tour.step2_body: Every subfolder of www/ becomes a site at <name>.test. Check HTTPS to issue a certificate via mkcert on first use.
tour.step3_body: Click Check all to compare installed versions against the latest published by each upstream project.
tour.config_desc: Replay the welcome tour highlighting the main UI hotspots.

mariadb.backup_needs_running: Start MariaDB to back up databases.
mariadb.backup_delete_confirm: Delete file {filename}? This cannot be undone.
